features.evidence: 99 that is just 200 more than the base m3 macbook pro and the same price if you were to spec up the bas m3 to 16 gigs of ram and of course with the m3 pro you get 18 gb of ram compared to 16 if you were to spec up the bas m3 along with three extra cpu cores four extra gpu cores and the new space black color the only advantage that the m3 has over the m3 pro is going to be in battery life and this is something that i ve noticed a bigger difference than i expected over the past month and i thought the first week the first couple of weeks were an anomaly but i ve noticed i got at least four additional hours out of the m3 compared to the m3 pro and i know it s seems ridiculous apple rates it for just 3 hours longer but i ve noticed on average 3 and 1 half to 4 hours consistantly on the m3 i get longer battery life on the m3 compared to the m3 pro and that could be a big deal for a lot of people if you re always on the go and you re traveling a lot you may not always have access to a charger so that s something you also have to take into account here but all in all i do think that you should avoid buying the base model m3 macbook pro but not just because of the 8 gb of ram i personally think that that is the least of the deal breakers with this model compared to the m3 pro so i never had any issues again with the 8 gigs of ram compared to 16 or 18 but i think there are four reasons that i will be returning the base m3 reason number one is the fact that we can only hook up one external monitor with the m3 compared to two with the m3 pro number two is the fact that we only have one fan in the m3 compared to two fans in the m3 pro this isn t a huge deal but i mentioned earlier for longevity and when you re doing those intensive tasks this is going to help cool down the macbook and just keep it healthy over time number three is the fact that we do not have the space black option for the base m3 you only get that with the m3 pro and max and then of course number four is the price especially when you take into account the amazon prices compared to the apple prices and i do also want to mention that apple does sell the bas m3 macbook pro on amazon as well for 450 making the price difference 350 between the two as opposed to 400 if you were to buy through apple but honestly if you re looking for a deal on a base macbook pro with 8 gb of ram you honestly probably would be better off just waiting for the m3 macbook air because if you re only looking for the base model device and you re just wanting to get the cheapest price the best deal you probably don t need all the power that a macbook pro in general has so for you i would say to just wait for the new macbook air coming next year however if you currently have an m2 macbook pro or a higher spec m1 pro or m1 max macbook pro i personally think that you should skip the m3 i just don t think it s worth it to upgrade to any of the m3 macbook pros because you re not going to really see a big difference in performance or even battery life for that matter so i think the only reason you should do it is if you re trying to minimize your loss because you got to think that next year when a new macbook pro comes out the value of your m1 macbook pro is just going to go down even further even m2 is going to go down even further so that s one thing to keep in mind and that s why i personally will be trading in i will be selling i should say my m1 max macbook pro the absolute beast i m going to be selling that and using that money to put towards an m3 pro macbook pro in space black this same one right here but of course i will be returning this and getting it off of amazon for 200 cheaper so yeah that is my experience with the m3 and the m3 pro macbook pros let me know your thoughts down in the comment section below if you enjoyed this video i would appreciate if you give it a thumbs up also be sure to subscribe for a lot more mac coverage just this but anyways guys i thanks see for watching and i ll see you soon

i ve spent the past month going back and forth between the m3 and the m3 pro macbook pros and i am stumped so in this video we re going to discuss why you might be making a mistake buying the base m3 macbook pro but why you also should not be paying extra for ram upgrades it s confusing so hopefully this video helps you clear things up and also helps you understand which m3 macbook pro you should buy let s start with the design because the introduction of the big m3 macbook pro led to the demise of the previous entry level macbook pro which was the 13 in model with the touch bar and now the new entrylevel pro macbook from apple comes with a more modern boxy design with the beautiful 14

i ve spent the past month going back and forth between the m3 and the m3 pro macbook pros and i am stumped so in this video we re going to discuss why you might be making a mistake buying the base m3 macbook pro but why you also should not be paying extra for ram upgrades it s confusing so hopefully this video helps you clear things up and also helps you understand which m3 macbook pro you should buy let s start with the design because the introduction of the big m3 macbook pro led to the demise of the previous entry level macbook pro which was the 13 in model with the touch bar and now the new entrylevel pro macbook from apple comes with a more modern boxy design with the beautiful 14

2 in liquid retina mini led display with that glorious 120 hz refresh rate along with the slim down bezels and the display is nearly identical to the previous m1 and m2 pro macbook pros except for the fact that the brightness is now 100 nits higher at 600 nits of peak brightness for sdr content and of course all of the m3 macbook pros have the same display this time however the first big downside with the base model m3 compared to the m3 pro is the lack of ports we only have two usbc ports on the base m3 macbook pro and i m somebody who runs my final cut pro projects from an x internal ssd which means that one of those spots is always going to be taken when i m working from my macbook pro now if i also have my device hooked up to an external display well there you go now both my ports are completely taken and if you re me when you re at home you are always hooked up to an external display but if you re not and if you re in the market for a new external display today s sponsor binq makes one of the best monitors in its price range this is their 2 7 in 4k designer monitor which offers seamless mac integration with a single usbc cable that also powers your macbook at 90 ws and thanks to their aq color technology the picture that you get from this monitor is excellent we have the industry standard 95 color gamut with p3 support along with delta e values less than three straight out of the box and that just means extremely accurate colors and they even give you this paper in the box showing what your specific monitor tested at but my favorite thing about this monitor is the aesthetic this thing just looks so clean and so good on the desk and it s super simple to set up as well and it s all thanks to the ergo armst on the back this thing just clamps down onto your desk it has built in cable management and it gives you all of the viewing flexibility that your heart would desire we do also get a second gen hotkey puck in the box which allows you to switch between multiple devices eas easily with the built in kvm switch so if you re in the market for a new monitor i do not think you can go wrong with this 27in bq designer monitor especially when you can get it for under 600 if you use my code linked down in the description below so if you want to pick one up for yourself be sure to hit that link down in the description below and also be sure to use the code to get that price under 600 bucks so with all that being said the ports are not the only thing that the m3 has less of than the m3 pro we also have less fans inside so the bas m3 only has one fan inside to cool it down whereas the m3 pro has two fans inside now i have not had to deal with any type of thermal throttling or anything even remotely close to that so this is not going to be a big deal to the majority of people but it is good to keep in mind if you are planning to push your macbook to extremes especially if you plan on having it for a very long time now also the bas m3 only allows you to have a single display output so you can only display to one external monitor at up to 6k at 60 hz whereas on the m3 pro you can hook up two external displays up to 8k at 60 hz so me personally i work off of a dual monitor setup so that instantly eliminates the m3 from something i would use on a day to day basis because i need my two displays up there but if that s not a deal breaker for you the per performance between these two might be look i work in final cut pro i work in lightroom i work in photoshop i work in audacity i work on a lot of creative applications really heavy applications and i ve not had a single issue with the base m3 machine despite only having 8 gb of ram and i compared the m3 to the m3 pro when under extreme load and both of them went into memory swap but they both performed nearly identical despite that so you can see here the task was exporting a 2hour long 4k video with a lightroom project opened and working on it along with rapidly going through several google chrome tabs because google chrome doesn t run as good on the mac so i was really trying to push both of these computers and they performed pretty much the same there was hardly no difference between the two and this was sustained for over an hour i was doing tests this and keep in mind this is an extreme test something that most people are not even going to ever do in the life of their macbook pro so for me personally with the work that i do which is photo editing video editing and potentially even more google chrome all that 8 gbt of ram is enough for me for 99

apple s lower cost laptops are also their most popular with the macbook air being their number one bestseller and it may not surprise you to hear that the number two bestseller is the base model macbook pro however that was at the former 1,300 price tag with this new m3 macbook pro replacing it at a higher 1600 starting price point of course that model does come with more power a better screen a new design with more ports and yeah it is a more premium product but it is priced pretty far away from something the entry level macbook air and it is 300 more expensive than that old m2 macbook pro which has me questioning who is this macbook pro really for and is this apple s biggest mistake in terms of a pricing strategy or really when you add up the entire value of this macbook pro is this possibly the best laptop that apple has ever made now we all know macbook pro has a lot of use cases and it connects us to a wider world world and one of the things i ve been doing lately is checking out the connected ai power world of meta sites where you can explore a bustling metropolis listen to music or play a few games while conversing with inhabitants that aren t just regular npcs with scripted responses but an actual ai personality that lives and works in the city that shares unique and interesting conversations and you can have your own unique experiences by visiting shops for a luxury boutique shopping experience and browse the unique car collections and then c customize your own car to your liking complete with the perfect paint job and then take it for a test drive through this metropolis i love taking in the views of the big city behind the wheel of the car and then glancing up through the sunroof and just getting an amazing view of all of the tall buildings that fill the city or i can take on the views of the amazing galleries that is available in meta sites with views that just aren t possible in a regular gallery meta sites is packed with so many things to do and different areas to visit your own personal hub complete with your own home in the the suburbs and relaxing areas with vibrant landscapes where you can enjoy tranquil moments a beautiful sunset this is an entirely new platform full of exciting developments and unique experiences so unleash the power of zola meta sites through the link in the description and be the first to get a chance to explore a unique virtual experience interact with npcs based on gpt and check out hyper realistic graphics on unreal engine 5 and thank you to z meta sites for sponsoring this video okay so we talked about how this model got refreshed and i think one of the things you have to look out for with any apple model refresh is did they add any value to the increase price of this product or did they just increase the price at a pure greed well it s easy to look at the outgoing model and the new model and see that these macs are quite different that s because this new macbook pro has more in common with the more expensive 2,000 version than it does with the previous m2 touch bar version the entire body has been reworked to have flatter sides overall it has a thicker appearance and it is slightly heavier in weight but it comes with a lot of benefits right it has more ports with an expanded port selection for a mag safe charging port an sd card slot an hdmi port and still retains the two thunderbolt ports from the previous model open up the macbook pro and you re no longer greeted by a touch bar on the top it s just a regular keyboard with a full row of function keys just every other mac and apple s lineup now so this macbook is no longer the ugly duckling it still has touch id in the top right corner to unlock your mac and of course typing on this mac just any other modern mac is a dream the keyboard feels great the trackpad is super premium and again the overall build quality on this laptop is just insane there is no other laptop in my opinion that feels as solid and as well built as these new macbook pros and every aspect of it feels precision engineered even the etching on the bottom that says macbook pro not only looks beautiful it feels beautiful when you run your finger across it small details this to me at least make me appreciate the product a whole lot more and every time i pick this up i m just reminded how premium of an experience this is so in terms of build quality compared to the macbook pro it s replacing it s a huge upgrade and it s not just build quality improvements you re getting expanded consumer level features as well i think the best is that new expanded 14 in display which just has less bezels around the entire display and not only is the display bigger and just looks better because of those reduced bezels but it s also a huge upgrade in screen technology this features a 120hz display refresh rate which makes everything you do from simple things just moving the mouse cursor to scrolling in websites animations and if you re playing games that can hit higher than 60 frames per second you get ultra smooth looking gameplay as well the new display somehow makes your old mac feels slow if you don t have a fast refresh rate mac and that really has nothing to do with performance it s just the refresh rate on the display the visual quality of the display is also one of the best in any laptop because of the mini led technology this uses an lcd panel but instead of big back lay leds it uses 10,000 smaller mini leds that can be turned on or off in controlled zones why does this matter well that means the display can turn off the zones in darker areas of the picture or increase the brightness of a zone for brighter areas in the picture this results not only in deeper black levels but the display can reach an amazing 1, 1600 nits of peak brightness during hdr playback for a really impressive amount of dynamic range when you re watching hdr video and that those black levels for those darker zones just means when you take the whole dynamic range into context you re getting a million to one contrast ratio in on scientific terms this display is amazing it is hands down the best laptop display i have used overall for a combination of high resolution refresh rate black levels brightness and hdr capabilities when i look at other high end windows laptops in these categories they usually check a few check boxes right it ll usually have either high resolution or a bright display or a really good contrast ratio but it s very rare to find something that really just checks every single box and this macbook pro does it i m telling you when you see this display it s hard to go back to something else else now this isn t new right these are upgrades that have been on the more expensive pro and max models of the macbook pro for the past 2 years but now they re here at the entry level model and that s important because it means you no longer have to spend 2,000 to get a macbook that has more ports or one with such an excellent display as this one but listen 1600 isn t exactly what i would classify as low cost or even entry level but is this value here better than the older macbook pro that s the thing i wanted to answer and i think it is and that s partly because of the starting specification of this macbook pro which starts with 512 gb of storage which is double the previous starting storage found on the m2 macbook pro so if you did the same storage upgrade on that macbook pro well this macbook pro is only 100 more expensive so when you account for all the upgrades you re getting for this model for 100 more that is a very little price increase for such a huge huge quality of life upgrade to a lot of these features but again we also have to temper our expectations down a little bit because despite this taking on the starting role of the 14 inch macbook pro it isn t a direct replacement of those higher end models because this doesn t come with the heavy hitting m3 pro or m3 max chips found on those more expensive models just the previous m2 model it replaces it comes with a nonpr branded m3 chip and that s 1,600 the big question is can this lower end m3 chip really hold its own against powerful laptop chips in its price category well the silicon here is really impressive single core performance is pretty much the highest you re going to get on any laptop or desktop in the world right now and it s an impressive bump even over the previous m2 chip with around a 15 performance gain multi core performance is also an impressive jump up and it s holding its own against the previous 22,000 base model m2 pro chip so the value for performance here is pretty good the flaws of the m3 chip are they ve always been for any of the base level chips the gpu performance just isn t there despite some additions hardware raate tracing these base m3 chips still aren t gpu workhorses still if you can find games that are optimized for apple silicon resid evil village you can get some pretty decent graphics quality and some stable frame rates for titles resid evil village but again titles resident evil village that are optimized for the mac and can take advantage of apple silicon are very few and far between i ll just say there s not that many of them available on the mac and then for other gpu heavy workloads 3d modeling and blender and stuff that this isn t the machine you want but still going with a regular m3 chip does have its perks and that is with efficiency and battery life gains when we re talking about a consumer level laptop this thing is amazing during my whole whole month testing this machine it has never gotten hot and i have never heard the fans spin up on it once with my workloads and the battery life on this lower end model is more than apple s more expensive 14 in m3 pro and m3 max models and it matches the battery performance of the ultra expensive 16 in macbook pro which has a way bigger battery in it but because the chip is so efficient despite having a smaller battery it gets that same 22 hour battery life rating now in real world usage this battery life rating is probably closer to around maybe 15 to maybe 20 hours of real world usage on this macbook pro again it s very dependent on what tasks you re doing but again battery life on this is fantastic it ll easily last you through a day and then standby time is also really good you can close this up come back to it days later and it s still going to hold a charge pretty close to where you left it and that experience of just having all this battery life on a laptop not having to worry about charging it really just leads to a great experience with this machine now all that s great and honestly a lot of the things i talked about this laptop are great especially for that 1,600 price point i really think this is a good laptop and honestly the m3 chip it s it s performing it belongs in this pro lev laptop but that s not really the issue for me here the issue here and this is the elephant in the room that we really didn t discuss yet is that 8 gb of starting memory for this configuration now i want to keep this short and i want to just tell her my recommendations in the shortest way possible the performance for the 8 gb in this machine surprised me it was pretty good i ingested some of the footage here i walked away for a bit and started editing this video doing the first draft here and it performed pretty well now listen i ve been reviewing apple silicon machines for 3 years now so i know how maybe the limitations of this system and i was okay this is doing better than i remember but as i ingested more footage into it as i put multiple streams of 4k as i extended the project to around 40 minutes in length then i could see dropped frames i could see that the system was having trouble with the memory pressure so is 8 gbes a miracle and is it going to is it going to be fine for every workflow of course not there s a reason why apple sells higher end configurations with more memory that s not a scam right more memory is going to mean better performance especially when you re using higher end applications but if you re doing basic tasks on this model and i really think this model in particular this bas level model is going to appeal to people that would be fine with a macbook air but want features the better screen maybe they want the bigger 14 in screen maybe they want more ports maybe they want the better speakers right you re getting a lot here especially with the increase in starting storage that when you look at it compared to a macbook air it s not that much more money to step up to this sometimes right and and that s great but also for pro level users who are thinking maybe i m going to get a super good deal on this model i would say there s some caution here and it s really dependent on your workflow there might be some people that could buy this laptop and if if you re not doing anything that s super intensive even as a pro level user maybe you re just editing short form video or something that maybe you d be fine with 8 gbes but i think the safe recommendation here is to to get that price under 600 bucks so with all that being said the ports are not the only thing that the m3 has less of than the m3 pro we also have less fans inside so the bas m3 only has one fan inside to cool it down whereas the m3 pro has two fans inside now i have not had to deal with any type of thermal throttling or anything even remotely close to that so this is not going to be a big deal to the majority of people but it is good to keep in mind if you are planning to push your macbook to extremes especially if you plan on having it for a very long time now also the bas m3 only allows you to have a single display output so you can only display to one external monitor at up to 6k at 60 hz whereas on the m3 pro you can hook up two external displays up to 8k at 60 hz so me personally i work off of a dual monitor setup so that instantly eliminates the m3 from something i would use on a day to day basis because i need my two displays up there but if that s not a deal breaker for you the per performance between these two might be look i work in final cut pro i work in lightroom i work in photoshop i work in audacity i work on a lot of creative applications really heavy applications and i ve not had a single issue with the base m3 machine despite only having 8 gb of ram and i compared the m3 to the m3 pro when under extreme load and both of them went into memory swap but they both performed nearly identical despite that so you can see here the task was exporting a 2hour long 4k video with a lightroom project opened and working on it along with rapidly going through several google chrome tabs because google chrome doesn t run as good on the mac so i was really trying to push both of these computers and they performed pretty much the same there was hardly no difference between the two and this was sustained for over an hour i was doing tests this and keep in mind this is an extreme test something that most people are not even going to ever do in review_summary: 99 that is just 200 more than the base m3 macbook pro and the same price if you were to spec up the bas m3 to 16 gigs of ram and of course with the m3 pro you get 18 gb of ram compared to 16 if you were to spec up the bas m3 along with three extra cpu cores four extra gpu cores and the new space black color the only advantage that the m3 has over the m3 pro is going to be in battery life and this is something that i ve noticed a bigger difference than i expected over the past month and i thought the first week the first couple of weeks were an anomaly but i ve noticed i got at least four additional hours out of the m3 compared to the m3 pro and i know it s seems ridiculous apple rates it for just 3 hours longer but i ve noticed on average 3 and 1 half to 4 hours consistantly on the m3 i get longer battery life on the m3 compared to the m3 pro and that could be a big deal for a lot of people if you re always on the go and you re traveling a lot you may not always have access to a charger so that s something you also have to take into account here but all in all i do think that you should avoid buying the base model m3 macbook pro but not just because of the 8 gb of ram i personally think that that is the least of the deal breakers with this model compared to the m3 pro so i never had any issues again with the 8 gigs of ram compared to 16 or 18 but i think there are four reasons that i will be returning the base m3 reason number one is the fact that we can only hook up one external monitor with the m3 compared to two with the m3 pro number two is the fact that we only have one fan in the m3 compared to two fans in the m3 pro this isn t a huge deal but i mentioned earlier for longevity and when you re doing those intensive tasks this is going to help cool down the macbook and just keep it healthy over time number three is the fact that we do not have the space black option for the base m3 you only get that with the m3 pro and max and then of course number four is the price especially when you take into account the amazon prices compared to the apple prices and i do also want to mention that apple does sell the bas m3 macbook pro on amazon as well for 450 making the price difference 350 between the two as opposed to 400 if you were to buy through apple but honestly if you re looking for a deal on a base macbook pro with 8 gb of ram you honestly probably would be better off just waiting for the m3 macbook air because if you re only looking for the base model device and you re just wanting to get the cheapest price the best deal you probably don t need all the power that a macbook pro in general has so for you i would say to just wait for the new macbook air coming next year however if you currently have an m2 macbook pro or a higher spec m1 pro or m1 max macbook pro i personally think that you should skip the m3 i just don t think it s worth it to upgrade to any of the m3 macbook pros because you re not going to really see a big difference in performance or even battery life for that matter so i think the only reason you should do it is if you re trying to minimize your loss because you got to think that next year when a new macbook pro comes out the value of your m1 macbook pro is just going to go down even further even m2 is going to go down even further so that s one thing to keep in mind and that s why
